task_completion.description: Create the Directory.Packages.props file at the solution root.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Solution Explorer in Visual Studio shows a file named 'Directory.Packages.props' at the root level of the EcommercePlatform solution, or an editor tab is open with this file path.

task_completion.description: Enable central package management in the props file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of Directory.Packages.props are visible in the editor and contain the XML property <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>.

task_completion.description: Define the correct versions for Newtonsoft.Json and Dapper in the props file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Directory.Packages.props file contains <PackageVersion Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" /> and <PackageVersion Include="Dapper" Version="2.1.35" /> (or equivalent valid MSBuild syntax).

task_completion.description: Update project files to remove local version attributes for the centrally managed packages.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The .csproj files for Ecommerce.Web, Ecommerce.Services, and/or Ecommerce.Data are open and show <PackageReference Include="Newtonsoft.Json" /> and <PackageReference Include="Dapper" /> WITHOUT any 'Version' attribute. Alternatively, the Visual Studio NuGet Package Manager UI shows these packages managed centrally.

task_completion.description: Ensure the solution builds successfully with the unified versions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Visual Studio Output window or bottom status bar displays a successful build message, such as 'Build: 3 succeeded, 0 failed' or 'Build succeeded'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Visual Studio 2022 IDE to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the Visual Studio 2022 interface being used to edit files and build the solution, rather than a generic text editor or purely terminal commands.

integrity.description: The build success message is from the actual Visual Studio build process.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Build succeeded' message appears in the standard Visual Studio Output pane or status bar, not typed into a text file or mocked in a separate window.

integrity.description: The agent modified the correct solution and project files.
integrity.visual_evidence: File paths visible in editor tooltips, tabs, or the Solution Explorer correspond to 'C:\Users\Docker\source\repos\EcommercePlatform' and its subprojects.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The solution directory is C:\Users\Docker\source\repos\EcommercePlatform. The expected versions to be defined in Directory.Packages.props are Newtonsoft.Json 13.0.3 and Dapper 2.1.35.
